name: Castile-La Mancha (Spain)

colors.symbolism: the crimson of the historic Crown of Castile
the white shields of the Order of Saint John (Knights Hospitaller) of La Mancha
the door, windows and details of the castle

description: Vertically divided in two equal halves by a vertical line: the hoist half crimson and bearing a golden three-towered castle in the centre; the fly half plain white.
history: Adopted by Article 5 of the Statute of Autonomy of Castile-La Mancha (Organic Law 9/1982 of 10 August). The crimson half with the golden castle recalls the historic Crown of Castile; the plain white fly represents the territories of the Order of Saint John (Knights Hospitaller) and the Order of Santiago, whose lordships covered much of La Mancha during the Reconquista.

features.symbolism: Vertically divided in two equal halves: the hoist half crimson with a golden castle (representing the historic Crown of Castile), the fly half white (representing the white shields of the Knights of Saint John associated with La Mancha).

features.role: the golden three-towered castle of Castile
features.symbolism: the historic arms of the Crown of Castile, recalling the medieval kingdom that gave its name to the region.
